title: pedicularis oederi subsp. genuina
intro: Pedicularis oederi subsp. genuina, also known as Lousewort, is a flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically grasses and sedges. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, while also being used as an expectorant to relieve respiratory congestion.
